description: Figure 11 presents electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) characterization of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) after potentiostatic polarization in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution, comprising three panels: (a) Nyquist plot showing capacitive arcs, (b) Bode magnitude plot of impedance modulus versus frequency, and (c) Bode phase plot of phase angle versus frequency. The data compare four samples: S0 (as-rolled without solution treatment), S950 (solution-treated at 950°C), S1050 (1050°C), and S1150 (1150°C). Panel (a) reveals S950 exhibits the largest capacitive arc radius while S0 shows the smallest, indicating superior oxide film density and stability for S950. In panel (b), the low-frequency impedance modulus (0.01 Hz) decreases with increasing grain size, with S950 maintaining the highest value. Panel (c) shows S950 maintains a higher phase angle over a wider frequency range, confirming a more uniform oxide film structure. The equivalent circuit diagram (Rs(Q1(R1(Q2R2)))) in (a) was used for fitting. These results demonstrate that grain size optimization via 950°C solution treatment yields the highest polarization resistance (21355 Ω·cm²) and optimal corrosion resistance through formation of a dense Al-rich oxide film.
